mechanism_narrative: A rising FEDFUNDS rate over recent months tightens financial conditions and lifts discount rates, which should pressure equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if FEDFUNDS increases are followed by positive 20d S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases over any 3-year rolling window, or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation remains elevated while FEDFUNDS is still below the inflation level, real policy rates are negative and the market should anticipate further tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation exceeds FEDFUNDS by more than 1.0 percentage point
failure_condition: Reject if the spread condition is met but 5d forward returns are positive in more than half of triggered observations, or if the signal loses directional accuracy over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A low and stable UNRATE signals resilient labor income and supports consumer spending, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if low-UNRATE periods fail to outperform high-UNRATE periods on 60d returns, or if the sign of the conditional return reverses for two consecutive annual windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in PAYEMS combined with a falling UNRATE indicates accelerating hiring without labor deterioration, which should lift cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS rises by more than 150 thousand over 2 months while UNRATE falls by at least 0.2 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce negative 20d returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus PAYEMS or UNRATE alone.

mechanism_narrative: Improving retail sales in RSAFS reflects stronger current consumption and revenue momentum, which should be followed by positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if rising RSAFS is not associated with higher 20d forward returns than flat or declining RSAFS, or if the relationship is unstable across successive 18-month windows.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO signals weakening industrial activity and rising recession risk, which should weigh on equity earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month declines in INDPRO do not precede weaker 60d returns, or if the average return after the trigger is not below the unconditional average by at least one standard error.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in HOUST signals housing demand weakening and reduced cyclical momentum, which should lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 5% versus its 3-month average
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST drawdowns are not followed by lower 20d S&P 500 returns than non-drawdown periods, or if the signal has no directional edge after accounting for monthly seasonality.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth is positive while RSAFS is also expanding, broad demand is accelerating across both production and consumption, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint strength condition does not outperform either proxy alone on 60d returns, or if the combined signal turns negative during multiple growth-expansion episodes.
